hypothesis: For the finite single-cell Rule 30 history c_0 through c_32767, direct CUDA row evolution returns exactly the independent packed CPU result; timings determine whether CUDA is faster for this exact workload.
interpretation: The complete 32768-byte center prefix and 1024-word final row matched exactly in the warm-up and all five measured CPU/CUDA repetitions. For this workload, CUDA was slower: its 280.757527 ms median end-to-end time exceeded the packed CPU median of 36.820499 ms. The 32767 sequential kernel launches dominate this direct single-history formulation.
limitations: This is one finite N=32768 single-history workload on one CPU/GPU host; it makes no claim about infinite Rule 30 behavior.
The timing result is workload-specific and is neither an asymptotic complexity result nor a lower bound for computing c_n.
The CPU comparator is an independent single-threaded packed uint64_t implementation of the same direct row-evolution task.
One CPU warm-up and one GPU warm-up preceded five measured repetitions; frequency scaling and unrelated host activity were not disabled.

Kernel-sequence timing covers the dependent CUDA launches, transfer timing covers explicit host/device transfers, and end-to-end timing additionally includes allocation, setup, synchronization, and cleanup in the CUDA API call.
The 39 C benchmark-session readings and 40 C final idle reading are coarse snapshots, not a per-repetition thermal trace; no clock, voltage, fan, power, or thermal control was changed.
Compute Sanitizer 2026.2.1 could not initialize its WSL/WDDM debugger interface, so this milestone has passing functional tests but no successful Compute Sanitizer run.

parameters.bit_order: center bytes c_0 through c_(N-1); final packed-row bit i is coordinate i-(N-1)

proof_scope: Exact finite equality of every center byte and final packed-row word between the independent CPU oracle and CUDA in the warm-up and each of five measured runs; all performance conclusions are empirical and workload-specific.
question: problem2

software.algorithm: direct packed uint64_t Rule 30 row evolution with one dependent CUDA kernel launch per row after the seed
